reviews.review_title: S p o o k y 🎃👻
reviews.review_description: Some other reviews were making me think this would be a bad game, but this is one of the best horror party games ive played! Keep on being awesome, and go have some Thrills, and Chills!
Not very interactive, not scary, very boring.
Do not buy

This game is best when you can share the headset with a friend and set up traps for them. In multiplayer mode you all have headset at same time and can set traps for the one person to experience the haunted house on their way to get the keys to exit the haunted house. I like how they have it customized to smooth or snap turning whoever you prefer. Not sure how active the public lobbies will be for a horror game like this but use their discord to try to find other players for multiplayer if you have friends to try it out with. I don't scare easily but had fun turning into one of the costume characters they have with a touch of a button. Has a great concept.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing the game and for your awesome review - much appreciated!  We're glad you had fun playing 👻

reviews.review_description:
You Can’t Scare Me is a playful twist on the horror genre, making it an engaging experience best enjoyed with friends. Designed best for multiplayer action, the game allows up to four players to dive into eight uniquely themed levels, from an eerie cemetery to a creepy cabin in the woods. The premise is simple yet entertaining: one player takes on the role of the “Haunted,” racing against time to collect hidden keys and bones, while the others are the 'Haunters,” who set audio and visual props to try to scare the Haunted, and earn parts from screams. While it may not send shivers down the spines of those unphased by jump scares, the sheer absurdity of the situations often leads to laughter and camaraderie among players.

Although the game is rated “Mature 17+,” it’s likely to become a hit among younger audiences, as its blend of humor and lighthearted frights creates a fun environment suitable for kids and families. The whimsical graphics and playful sound design further enhance the overall experience, making it accessible to those who may not typically gravitate toward horror games. For players looking to share some laughs and enjoy a friendly competition, You Can’t Scare Me is a delightful choice that provides plenty of entertaining moments, making it a worthwhile addition to any gaming collection.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the awesome review!  I appreciate you seeing the nuance between the humor and scares.  Goal is definitely for everyone to have fun getting scared! :)

reviews.review_description:
This was such a fun one to play with friends! Allowing up to 4 players, one person is to find objects while the others lay spooky traps to get em to scream! Score is kept, but to be honest I have no idea what our scores were- we all won. So many laughs were had, whether I was haunting or haunted. I will admit, I screamed more than I thought I would. 😝

The traps vary from scary figures to spooky sounds to creepy crawlers to frightening costumes. Use a variety and you are almost guaranteed to provide a hilarious startle to your friends. I look forward to the addition of more traps to keep the key hunter guessing. A great time to be had!!

Features pass and play so you can use just one headset and play with a friend!!

developer_response.body: What an awesome review to read- thank you so much and I'm so glad you hear you enjoyed the game!  Haha it is way more fun if you are easily startled.  We have an update planned for some new content too, stay tuned!  Thanks again and have a rad spooky season! 🎃

reviews.review_description:
While single player isn't bad, where this really shines is in multi-player.

Being able to be actively involved in the scares is honestly the best part - way better than being the person experiencing it.

They have this set for mature audiences but honestly, most of the fear comes from atmosphere and anticipating jump scares. It's not that intense but is fun, especially with kids... or grownups who've had a drink or two.

In the app store, I feel like there are games and then there are experiences and one of the downsides to experiences is the limited replay value. This kind of works as both and while it's not going to be the kind of thing you'll want to play again and again alone, it really is fun in a group setting and being able to change the scares around in the eight environments gives it way more repeatability than some of the things that offer a similar type of experience but lack a little bit of the gaming element this has.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for playing You Can't Scare Me and for leaving a review - we're really glad to hear you enjoyed the game!  Agreed - multiplayer mode is our favorite way to play too 😉  Thanks again for the kind words and happy gaming! 👻

reviews.review_description:
I believe this is another one of those solid party games to play with your friends. 
The game is definitely modeled to be played by all ages, there's nothing too hardcore and frankly I see it being most popular amongst younger audiences. You'll definitely have those guys screaming and fooling around as they scare each other and hey, specially during Halloween you're gonna need some of those fun scare party tricks!  
As far as the game goes, while it does offer a solo player variant, it truly shines in it's multiplayer. Both laughs and screams were shared amongst my friends, total blast. As mentioned before, it is definitely not a game full blown aimed at giving you nightmares, it's all about the fun thrill. I see that as a big advantage to share this type of fun with people who are less willing to play horror, being scaredy cats. 
As with everything else, the more the merrier, specially in horror and screams! 
Give it a shot, a scream you won't forget.

developer_response.body: Heya!  Thanks so much for playing, and for taking the time to leave a rad review!  Really glad to hear you enjoyed playing with your friends - hope you all got some good scares in!  Thanks for your feedback about the fear level/ages too - much appreciated.  Anyway thank you again for playing, psyched to hear you had fun and have a great week! 👻

reviews.review_description: You can’t scare me is a fun game best enjoyed with friends. One person gets to try to walk around the map while the others try to scare that person. Scores are rewarded depending on how much noise the microphone on your quest detects. The more you gasp, scream, or chuckle, the better score the opponents will get. Your job as the person being scared is to collect all the necessary keys as well as optional bones before making your way out of the location you’re playing in. The job of the people scaring you is to pick their scary character, and select an assortment of props to try to scare you. The props can be noises, traps, or scary figures that move when you get close. While the game has its moments where things can pop out, it isn’t the scariest once you know what to expect. The darker maps are better for surprises, but it’s also more difficult to navigate because it’s harder to see. I find this game best to be played as a social hangout with your friends. We found ourselves laughing more than being scared, but it was a good time nonetheless.

developer_response.body: Thanks very much for playing You Can't Scare Me!  We're thrilled to hear you had a good time playing with your friends.  We also appreciate your feedback on the lighting/scary levels and will take it into consideration.  Thanks again for playing and we appreciate your review! 👻

reviews.review_description: I playtested months ago and thought it was half baked, tried it again to be sure and felt the same. I was hoping that somehow that with the fall release that some sort of progress was made to bring the game to a better level. I was wrong, dead wrong. First off, it is trying to lean into the idea of a scary party game, with the focus being on the party aspect and not scares so you get a lot of cheap jumpscares instead of anything cool and built up. It plays like an old android Slender game, even down to the objective of item collection to win. The assets are mostly unity horror main stays that you'll recognize immediatly. This has a cool idea but everything in the department of execution is poor, it should have spent more time being developed and not pushed out asap.

reviews.review_title: This Krueger Needs More Time In the Oven
reviews.review_description:
Although the idea of playing the actors and setting the stage of your own haunted house is a great one, You Can't Scare Me falls falls short in a few critical areas hampering the fun. The game's undead spirit is in the right place with the goal being to terrify or endure horrors envisioned by you and your friends. Ghoulish obstacles, wearable costumes, and place-able monstrosities are all at your disposal while one unlucky soul attempts to find keys in an attempt to escape their torment.

Unfortunately, you'll be hard pressed to find original scares here with most of the art being comprised of store assets and feeling generally uninspired. Unlocking new levels and implements of terror are great in concept but sadly suffer a similar fate.  If these don't scare you then the random black screen crashes, performance hitches, and a rabid plague of physics issues might just.
I think that if the playable character was actually in peril or at the risk of scaring to death that there would be more fun to be had here.

I truly want to love this Frankenstein of a game however, until a number of these issues are addressed I just can't recommend to any but the most forgiving and jumpy of haunted house goers.

developer_response.body:
Heya- thanks very much for playing the game and leaving your feedback.  We're very glad you like the concept of the game!  We're working on some bug patches to be rolled out soon, but we'd love to hear more about the specific crashes/performance issues you had with the game so we can try and fix them up.
If you have a chance, please drop us a line with more information (what preceded the bug, which headset you have) at mail@lunabeat.com or let us know on our Discord, https://discord.gg/r3gjJyzyu4 .  We'd be very appreciative!
Thanks also for the suggestion - I like the idea of putting the Haunter in peril - it's been tossed around before, having a more certain death scenario, we may be able to look into it for an update.  Anyway thank you again for playing!

reviews.review_description: The controls work well on this game, it is easy to decide who is going to try to scare (the ghost) and the other 3 players are trying to find keys to escape the level. There are a lot of level choices, and smaller ones if you are looking for a quick game. There are so many options to find props to scare friends! Even if you are not easily scared this game can be a great laugh with friends. Perfect for the Halloween season!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for leaving such an awesome review, and we're really glad you enjoyed the game!  Appreciate it and we hope you have a wonderful spooky season! 👻

reviews.review_title: Great game to play with friends
reviews.review_description:
I've played some good hours with friends on this game !

I totally recommand playing this game :
- Asymetric gameplay where hunter place traps to scare survivors.
- Easy to play with unaware friends.
- No motion sickness
- Voice transmission enabled between players

My only regret is that's sad we can't play as hunter using mobile phone in party mode. So we can scare them live instead of preparing everything isolated fron them (so they didn't see what you place)

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for playing and for leaving a review!  Awesome to hear you enjoyed it :D  I love the idea of the phone app in party mode - we have talked about potentially doing something similar to this, I can't make any promises but keep your eyes on the updates ;)  Thank you again! 👻

reviews.review_description: Got scared like bad but so far fun +5⭐️ amazing scaring friends are most fun.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing and for the fang-tastic review!  It's much appreciated! 👻

reviews.review_description: "You Can't Scare Me" is a fun, approachable entry into VR horror that I feel focuses more on atmosphere than super scares, making it great for those who want a spooky vibe without too much panic! The environment feels eerie, and the sound design keeps you on edge.  It’s more of a suspenseful adventure than full-on horror, so you get the thrill of exploring creepy areas without feeling too scared. If you’re looking for a chill scare and a bit of spooky fun, this game is a great pick for a VR horror experience, especially with friends.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for playing You Can't Scare Me and for your spook-tacular review!  Appreciate your feedback - and glad to hear you liked exploring, we tried to put lots of creepy little nooks and crannies in!  Thanks again and have a fang-tastic week!

reviews.review_description:
This is not your typical horror game.  Wether you are a haunted or haunter there is plenty of enjoyment to be had.  There is a solo experience of you versus the computer which is nice but the game really shines with a group of friends.  
There is plenty of ways the haunter can try to get a scream out of their prey.  The traps are split into three categories of effects, sounds, and props.  The sounds can add a little extra to your set up while the effects are good for distractions.  The props can be used to try and get those jump scare points.  You can join the fun yourself by putting on a costume and trying to scare them yourself.   
  The haunted has one goal make it through the map gather 3 keys and collect some bones for bonus points.  Beware though your mic is worst nightmare.  Every time you “scream” that’s a point for the other team.
  The games have a variety of 8 maps ranging from a classroom to a mansion.  
If your looking for a super scary nightmare infused game this is not it but I will guarantee a few laughs and good times with your group.

developer_response.body: Heya!  Thanks so much for playing and for leaving a cool review!  Glad you had fun with the game, esp with the multiplayer mode!  We will be changing up some of the description of the game soon so it's clearer it's a fun Halloween-themed party game, and not an ultra-scary experience to give you nightmares. But we're really glad you had a good time playing and very much appreciate your nice review! :) Thank you very much!

reviews.review_description:
Very unique VR experience and perfect timing right before Halloween. 
Scare friends or be scared by friends in 8 different maps while you look for keys to escape.  
It’s not too scary or gory. More of a fun challenge to scary others with distractions and jump scares so their mic picks up some screams or shrieks. 
The music and sounds create a perfect unsettling atmosphere. The darker maps are scarier to me. Some props are great and creepy while others are not. 
Search YouTube for gameplay and you will see how fun it is when you play with others. 

The only downside I see is it shines with others. You need friends who play this type of game or a active community in the lobbyi

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for playing You Can't Scare Me and for sharing your feedback!  Great to hear you're enjoying the game 🎃 We're also working on an update that should help with public matchmaking to make it easier to play with others.  Thanks again for playing and leaving a review, we're monstrously grateful for your support! 👻

reviews.review_description: Really fun and a great horror game on both sides of the game.
I did like playing with friends but its not fully a game for me but i still recomend playing it and it is a good price

developer_response.body: Thanks for checking out the game, we appreciate your feedback! 😊

reviews.review_description:
So i read your reply and thanks for telling me that but you should DEFINETLEY add a quick play mode
Ñ.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your feedback and for updating your review!  A few players have requested a public match-making mode so we are definitely looking into making one in an update.  I love the quick-play mode idea too!  We'll see what we can do 🎃  Thank you again!
